title: Implement expand-migrate-contract DB migration strategy
summary: Review of changes addressing external review #2 feedback from berendt. Three items addressed: (1) W-001: Added Chainsaw E2E test suite under tests/e2e/keystone/upgrade-flow/ covering fresh deployment installedRelease tracking, sequential upgrade via expand-migrate-contract phases, and skip-level upgrade rejection. (2) W-002: Expanded inline comment in internal/common/job/job.go to explicitly acknowledge blast radius of Background propagation policy change and document that only the keystone operator uses RunJob. (3) I-001: Added IsDowngrade() function in version.go with distinct DowngradeNotSupported condition reason, separating downgrades from skip-level upgrades for clearer operator diagnostics at 3am.

tests_checklist.item: Unit tests exist and pass for all new code (IsDowngrade, TestInitiateUpgrade_DowngradeRejected)
IsDowngrade has comprehensive table-driven tests (7 cases: same-year lower minor, earlier year, earlier year same minor, same version, upgrade, year rollover, patch suffix)
Downgrade rejection integration test verifies both error message and condition reason
Chainsaw E2E test covers fresh deployment, sequential upgrade, and skip-level rejection
Existing tests unaffected (all controller and job package tests pass)
E2E test verifies expand/migrate/contract Jobs exist as proof of phased execution

code_quality_checklist.item: IsDowngrade follows same structure as IsSequentialUpgrade and IsPatchOnly (sibling consistency)
Downgrade check placed before sequential upgrade check in initiateUpgrade (fail-fast ordering)
Condition-setting pattern matches existing inline one-off transitions (consistent with I-002 finding)
job.go comment accurately documents blast radius scope
No dead code, no commented-out code, no magic strings

security_checklist.item: No user input handling in changed code (version strings come from CR spec, not external input)
PSS Restricted security context unaffected by changes

architecture_checklist.item: IsDowngrade is minimal and correct (year comparison + same-year minor comparison)
E2E test follows established Chainsaw pattern (SPDX header, step comments, JMESPath assertions, catch diagnostics, unique CR name, fixture ordering)
Blast radius of job.go change verified: only keystone operator calls RunJob (directly and via database.RunDBSyncJob wrapper)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated logic (IsDowngrade is a single-purpose function called from one location)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Downgrade detected before sequential upgrade validation (more specific error first)
Both error return and status condition set on downgrade detection

defensive_checklist.item: ParseRelease errors handled before IsDowngrade call (nil-safe)
ObservedGeneration set on new DowngradeNotSupported condition

suggested_improvements: I-001 [INFO]: internal/common/job/job.go:78-79 — Comment says 'only the keystone operator uses RunJob' but internal/common/database/database.go:166 also calls job.RunJob via RunDBSyncJob wrapper. The blast radius claim is effectively correct (only keystone's reconcile_database.go calls RunDBSyncJob), but a more precise phrasing would be 'only keystone operator reconciliation paths trigger RunJob'. No action required — current wording is adequate for its purpose.
next_steps: Merge after CI passes — all external review feedback items addressed
